goal: Surface phase2 check judgments in the view: sync.rebuildView consumes EventCheckJudgment, IntentView gains LastCheck summary, show/log display it. Without this, mainline check --submit is write-only — judgment events live in the actor log but no command can see them.

summary.title: Surface phase2 check judgments in the view (LastCheck)
summary.what: domain.IntentView gains an optional LastCheck *CheckSummary field. sync.rebuildView's event switch now handles EventCheckJudgment, populating LastCheck on the candidate intent (last-write-wins because the actor log streams chronologically). A new extractAgainstIntents helper pulls referenced mainline ids out of judgments, preferring per-evidence MainlineIntent and falling back to parsing 'task_<candidate>_<mainline>'. The CLI surfaces the rollup: `mainline show <id>` prints a 'Check:' line on view-path output; `mainline log` carries a [check:ok|!|?|-] marker per row, with the marker computed by query.go's checkMarker helper.
summary.why: rc4 dogfood after PR #11 was queued: I ran `mainline check --prepare` then `mainline check --submit` with a hand-crafted CheckJudgmentResult and got back event_id evt_fe66d4c8. Then I tried to find that judgment anywhere — `mainline show`, `mainline log`, `mainline status` — none of them surfaced it. Reading sync.go showed why: the event switch handles IntentSealed/Abandoned/Superseded/MergeAcknowledged but EventCheckJudgment is silently ignored. CheckSubmit was a write-only black hole. mainline check is the project's headline multi-agent feature; if an agent runs it and the verdict is unfindable, the whole feature is decorative.
summary.user_goal: Make mainline check --submit observable: every command that knows about an intent should also know whether (and how) it has been judged.

decisions.rationale: Co-locates the verdict with the rest of the intent's view-layer state (status, status_evidence, summary, fingerprint). All consumers (show, log, future check --history) load the view anyway; no extra read paths.
rejected: Standalone check_results store — duplicates the actor-log-events-rebuild-the-view pattern with a parallel pipeline; bigger surface area for drift.

Inline the entire Judgments array — would bloat the view JSON with deep evidence text from every check ever run; LastCheck is enough for status display, full history is recoverable from the actor log on demand.

decisions.chose: Last-write-wins (single CheckSummary, replaced on every later judgment)
decisions.rationale: An old 'no_conflict' verdict masking a freshly discovered conflict is the dangerous failure mode; keeping only the latest judgment makes that impossible. Full history remains recoverable from the actor log if needed.

decisions.chose: Prefer per-evidence MainlineIntent; fall back to parsing task_id
decisions.rationale: Evidence is self-describing and survives task_id format changes. task_id parse is the safety net for judgments emitted with empty Evidence (legitimate for no_conflict verdicts where the agent has nothing to point at). Belt and braces.
rejected: Add MainlineIntent to ConflictJudgment top-level — schema change without immediate value because existing CheckTask already carries it; reading the produced judgment is the only place where this matters.

decisions.rationale: Keeps the existing one-line log format readable. ! flags conflict, ? flags need-human-review (which dominates because human-review is a strictly stronger condition than has-conflict), - flags 'never checked'. Single-character is grep-friendly.

risks: Stale draft path on `mainline show <merged-intent>`: when a local draft for a now-merged intent still exists, show returns the draft (no LastCheck) instead of the view (has LastCheck). Pre-existing UX bug; not in this PR's scope.

If extractAgainstIntents gets a malformed task_id the fallback may produce a garbage 'int_' id; mitigated because malformed ids do not match any real intent in the view, just appear cosmetically as against= entries that no command resolves further.
followups: mainline check (no flags) → show the candidate intent's LastCheck plus links to the most recent task package and the agent prompt; closes the 'how do I see the latest verdict' loop.
mainline check --history <intent> → walk the actor log and print every CheckJudgmentEvent, not just the latest, for audit.
Fix show to prefer view over draft when the view's status is non-drafting, so already-merged intents show their LastCheck via the view path.

architectural_claims: EventCheckJudgment is now part of the rebuildView pipeline; CheckSubmit is no longer write-only.
IntentView.LastCheck is the view-layer surface for phase2 verdicts; UI commands consume only this field, not the actor log directly.
behavioral_changes: mainline show on a view-path intent now prints a Check line summarising the latest judgment.
mainline log carries a [check:ok|!|?|-] marker per row.
mainline sync now updates LastCheck on every fetch; older verdicts are overwritten by newer ones.

data_model_changes.name: domain.CheckSummary
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Optional pointer field on IntentView; older views deserialize cleanly with LastCheck nil. New writes populate it on the next sync.

migration_notes: Existing actor logs with prior CheckJudgmentEvent payloads are now picked up automatically by the next sync.
